title: Leptocionium denticulatum
taxonomy: Leptocionium denticulatum is a species of lichen in the family Collemataceae. It belongs to the kingdom Fungi, phylum Ascomycota, class Lecanoromycetes, and order Peltigerales. This species is classified under the genus Leptocionium. Leptocionium denticulatum is a type of lichen that grows on trees and other substrates. The classification of Leptocionium denticulatum helps scientists understand its relationships and characteristics within the plant kingdom.

It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in the treatment of various ailments, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
